mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects contracting manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods do not precede negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% across 100 historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real interest rates, measured by declining nominal federal funds rates alongside stable or falling consumer prices, boost equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL yields a negative or insignificant Rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no predictive power for 60-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, strong macroeconomic momentum confirms business cycle expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, invalidating the momentum transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase signals robust capacity utilization and labor absorption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of post-shock recovery periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant.
